title: Adaptive execplan section profiles and canonical core
goal: Implement GitHub #832 and #833 together: execplans should have a compact required core with task-shaped optional sections, and repeated planning projections should derive from canonical core fields instead of hand-maintained duplicates.

The lane is complete when scaffolding can select bounded, lane, delegation, high-assurance, and closeout section profiles without breaking existing plan compatibility, and checks or fixtures prevent divergent duplicate next-action/proof values.
non_goals: Do not rewrite historical archived plans.
Do not remove existing execplan fields until compatibility and projections are proven.
Do not broaden into unrelated planning backlog such as large-file hotspot remediation or weak-agent TypeScript routing.

intent.outcome: Execplan scaffolding uses a compact canonical core plus task-shaped optional sections, and derived views avoid repeated hand-maintained next-action/proof/continuation text.
intent.constraints: Keep scope bounded to GitHub #832 and #833; preserve compatibility with existing planning-execplan/v1 records.
intent.latitude: Use bounded evidence-backed changes that reduce plan authoring cost and continuation ambiguity for agents.
intent.escalation: Escalate when a better-looking fix requires a schema break, archive migration, or broad rewrite of planning projection code.

execution.next_step: Inspect execplan scaffolding, rendering, projection code, and fixtures, then define the canonical core plus task-shaped profiles before changing generation behavior.
execution.proof: Planning tests, planning surface checks, and focused scaffold/projection fixtures prove compatibility and duplicate-value prevention.

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Execplans should adapt to task shape without making agents maintain repeated fields that can drift.

iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: continue the current milestone until the completion criteria are met

intent_interpretation.literal request: Promote the next lane; clarified as GitHub #832 and #833 after finishing the active TypeScript lane.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Make execplans cheaper and safer for agents by adapting required fields to task shape and deriving repeated views from one canonical core.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Define bounded, lane, delegation, high-assurance, and closeout section profiles, plus canonical core/projection rules, without breaking existing plan compatibility.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Planning package scaffolding, rendering, projection code, planning tests and fixtures, active planning state, and this execplan.
execution_bounds.max changed files: About 12 files; stop and reassess if implementation needs broad schema migration or archive rewrites.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: make test-planning; make lint-planning; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace reconcile --format json
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before broadening beyond the promoted item.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, adjacent modules, or canonical contracts not named by this plan.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, the promoted item, and the narrow files needed for the current implementation step.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Active lane #832/#833: define task-shaped execplan profiles and canonical core projections while preserving planning-execplan/v1 compatibility.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Task-shaped execplan section profiles and canonical core projections for GitHub #832/#833.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Preserve existing plan compatibility and keep unrelated planning lanes deferred.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Profile taxonomy, fixture shape, projection tightening, and narrow compatibility checks.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A correct fix requires schema-breaking field removal, archive migration, or broad planning architecture rewrite.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve the larger intended outcome separately from this bounded slice.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice advance the declared larger outcome, or only complete the local task?

active_milestone.scope: Implement GitHub #832 and #833 as one coherent adaptive execplan-shape lane.

immediate_next_action: Inspect execplan scaffolding, rendering, projection code, and fixtures, then define the canonical core plus task-shaped profiles before changing generation behavior.
invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.
completion_criteria: Execplan scaffolding has a compact required core plus optional section groups selected by task shape, assurance level, delegation need, compliance risk, and closeout state.
Intent, scope, next action, proof requirements, continuation, and closeout have one authoritative source or a checked projection rule preventing divergent duplicate values.
Existing planning-execplan/v1 records remain readable and compatible.

execution_run.changed surfaces: none yet; execution has not changed files
execution_run.next step: Inspect execplan scaffolding, rendering, projection code, and fixtures, then define the canonical core plus task-shaped profiles.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Implement GitHub #832 and #833: adaptive execplan section profiles plus canonical execplan core/projection rules.

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.

drift_log: 2026-05-07: Promoted from TODO direct-task shape into an execplan.
